texto: Someone has said of books that they are our 'amplest heritages' of thought, and so they are. That doesn't necessarily mean that they must be learned or profound. They are food for the mind and different minds require different foods ; everyone is better for variety. Whatever stimulates the mind feeds it, be it fact, fiction or fable. That is where our responsibility lies ; in knowing what builds good mental blood and brawn, and in dispensing that only. Don't ever let yourself think you haven't time to read.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mary Virginia Provines sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Someone has said of books that they are our 'amplest heritages' of thought, and so they are. That…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, books, knowledge, útil em momentos de knowledge e librarians.

seo.descriptionSeo: Orientação direta de Mary Virginia Provines sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Someone has said of books that they are our 'amplest heritages'…

frase_original: Someone has said of books that they are our 'amplest heritages' of thought, and so they are. That doesn't necessarily mean that they must be learned or profound. They are food for the mind and different minds require different foods ; everyone is better for variety. Whatever stimulates the mind feeds it, be it fact, fiction or fable. That is where our responsibility lies ; in knowing what builds good mental blood and brawn, and in dispensing that only. Don't ever let yourself think you haven't time to read.

texto: There is a somewhat time-worn joke about people taking up library work because they like to read : the joke consisting of the fact that librarians have so little time to read. But, I tell you, those who do not, and there are some, are in the wrong profession.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mary Virginia Provines: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “There is a somewhat time-worn joke about people taking up library work because they like to read …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, books, joke pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e joke.

frase_original: There is a somewhat time-worn joke about people taking up library work because they like to read : the joke consisting of the fact that librarians have so little time to read. But, I tell you, those who do not, and there are some, are in the wrong profession.
